task_completion.description: Verify the diagram is a hierarchical tree starting with 'Home'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a tree-like diagram structure with a root node labeled 'Home' at the top or center, branching out to other pages.

task_completion.description: Verify all required pages are present in the diagram.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for nodes labeled with the following pages: Journal, Footwear, Apparel, Contact, Returns, Brands, and Camping.

task_completion.description: Verify low-traffic pages (under 100 views) are excluded from the diagram.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Ensure there are NO nodes labeled 'discontinued-boots', 'company-picnic', 'legacy-sitemap', or 'wipes' anywhere in the diagram.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct color coding is applied to the nodes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Blue (#dae8fc) for retained pages (e.g., Home, Contact, Returns, Camping), Green (#d5e8d4) for new pages (e.g., Journal, Brands), and Orange (#ffe6cc) for consolidated pages (e.g., Footwear, Apparel).

task_completion.description: Verify consolidated/redirected pages are labeled with their new URLs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Orange nodes (like Footwear and Apparel) and ensure they contain text indicating their new URL path.

task_completion.description: Verify the diagram was saved as a .drawio file and exported as a PDF in the correct directories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file explorer window, terminal output, or draw.io save dialog showing 'sitemap_architecture.drawio' in '~/Diagrams/' and 'sitemap.pdf' in '~/Diagrams/exports/'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a graphical diagramming tool (like draw.io) to create the sitemap.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the draw.io interface or a similar visual diagramming application being actively used.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply write raw XML/drawio code in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the .drawio file was not generated by typing raw XML into VS Code, nano, or vim.

integrity.description: Verify the PDF was exported using the application's export feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Export as PDF' dialog or menu action in the diagramming tool, rather than a terminal command just renaming a file extension to .pdf.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Required nodes to look for: Home, Journal (new), Footwear (consolidated), Apparel (consolidated), Contact, Returns, Brands (new), Camping (retained). Forbidden nodes (do not include): discontinued-boots, company-picnic, legacy-sitemap, wipes. Color mapping: Blue (#dae8fc) for retained pages, Green (#d5e8d4) for new pages, Orange (#ffe6cc) for consolidated/redirected pages. Consolidated pages (Orange) must include the new URL in their label. Expected output files: ~/Diagrams/sitemap_architecture.drawio and ~/Diagrams/exports/sitemap.pdf.
